takeaways: Fog-of-war patterns make uncertainty visible by obscuring unexplored or unverified areas.
Map upgrades should preserve clarity and performance while improving visual fidelity.

imageGeneration.prompt: Use case: stylized-concept. Asset type: website game module background texture for a static dashboard minigame. Primary request: premium dark game UI background for a Venue Mapper module about discovering online money venues, opportunity source cartography, payout clarity, and first-proof routes. Scene/backdrop: futuristic cartography table with glowing map tiles, fog-of-war patches, source beacons, route lines, compass scan rings, small treasure-like venue nodes, and faint tactical grid marks. Subject: abstract opportunity source map, beacons, route pins, scan cones, payout signal crystals; no people or characters. Style/medium: polished 3D game concept art, premium SaaS-game hybrid, clean enough for dashboard UI overlays. Composition/framing: wide landscape dashboard texture, darker open negative space through the center for cards and controls, richer detail around edges, no decorative border. Lighting/mood: quiet high-end control room glow, luminous teal and green scan light with warm amber payout highlights. Color palette: dark charcoal, deep teal, green, amber, faint blue, restrained violet accents. Materials/textures: glass map surface, matte metal trim, holographic fog, soft volumetric glow, crisp but not noisy. Constraints: no text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermark, no people, no brand marks, no currency symbols, avoid clutter in the center.

frontend: Added venue-mapper dispatch in renderGameView and gameStepCount.
Added buildVenueMapperSites to derive five map beacons from lane trail text, counts, artifacts, traces, service requests, blockers, and gates.
Added renderVenueMapperGame and renderVenueSite with focused beacon controls, clarity verdict, route map, fog sweeps, and site cards.
Added responsive Venue Mapper CSS for docked detail panels, stacked desktop, and mobile layouts.
